content_of_post_to_comment:
I recently turned 22, and I've wasted four years of my life. I had some mental issues and dropped out of community college. For the next three years, I worked but, like an idiot didn't save my money. After moving across the country with family and starting at a new community college, my parents wanted me to focus on school. Then after a couple of months, they wanted me to get a job, so I got a stupid vendor job where I stock cookies all day, which takes up most of my time.

When I arrive home at 8 or 9, I feel so exhausted. I secretly stopped going to classes since work took up so much of my schedule. A year passed, and my parents expect me to graduate next year, but I've barely started and have barely even saved $2,000. I don't know what to do besides offing myself. It's so pathetic and embarrassing. all my friends and everyone I knew from high school are graduating university while I've haven’t done anything with my life. I feel like theres no hope for me and that I ruined my life. I have no passions or aspirations, barely an education, no skills, no dreams, never had a girlfriend, have no friends, no nothing. I tried to do new hobbies but I suck at everything I do. Every time I look in the mirror underneath i see a pathetic fuck up.
comment: The fact that you’re working until 8 or 9, moved across the country, and still made it through a rough few years doesn’t sound like someone who did nothing. It sounds like someone who got overwhelmed and started hiding from the problem. Dropping classes was a mistake, but it’s a fixable one, not a life sentence. Also, when you say you don’t know what to do besides offing yourself, please don’t sit with that alone—reach out to someone you trust or a crisis line if those thoughts feel close. What’s one thing you could realistically change this month: work hours, classes, or talking honestly with your parents?
